channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. B
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review B
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Electrical readout of topological spin order in ultrathin insulators with small magnetization
item.description:
Author(s): Jing Li, Huilin Lai, Andrew H. Comstock, Aeron McConnell, Bharat Giri, Yu Yun, Tianhao Zhao, Xiao Wang, Yongseong Choi, Xuemei Cheng, Jian Shen, Zhigang Jiang, Dali Sun, Wenbin Wang, and Xiaoshan Xu

Here, the authors demonstrate that the interfacial topological Hall effect enables an electrical readout of topological spin order in insulating magnets with extremely small magnetization. In Pt/hexagonal LuFeO3 heterostructures, the spin topology from noncoplanar canting is imprinted onto Pt via magnetic proximity, producing a large Hall response detectable down to the ultrathin limit of 1.5 unit cells. This approach provides a sensitive probe of otherwise inaccessible magnetic order in ultrathin insulating systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134436] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jing Li, Huilin Lai, Andrew H. Comstock, Aeron McConnell, Bharat Giri, Yu Yun, Tianhao Zhao, Xiao Wang, Yongseong Choi, Xuemei Cheng, Jian Shen, Zhigang Jiang, Dali Sun, Wenbin Wang, and Xiaoshan Xu

Here, the authors demonstrate that the interfacial topological Hall effect enables an electrical readout of topological spin order in insulating magnets with extremely small magnetization. In Pt/hexagonal LuFeO3 heterostructures, the spin topology from noncoplanar canting is imprinted onto Pt via magnetic proximity, producing a large Hall response detectable down to the ultrathin limit of 1.5 unit cells. This approach provides a sensitive probe of otherwise inaccessible magnetic order in ultrathin insulating systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134436] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Electrical readout of topological spin order in ultrathin insulators with small magnetization
item.dc:creator: Jing Li, Huilin Lai, Andrew H. Comstock, Aeron McConnell, Bharat Giri, Yu Yun, Tianhao Zhao, Xiao Wang, Yongseong Choi, Xuemei Cheng, Jian Shen, Zhigang Jiang, Dali Sun, Wenbin Wang, and Xiaoshan Xu

item.title: Berry curvature induced giant anomalous and spin texture driven Hall responses in the layered kagome antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{GdTi}}_{3}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{4}$
item.description:
Author(s): Shobha Singh, Shivam Rathod, Rong chen, Lipika, Sneh, Rie Y. Umetsu, Yan Sun, and Kaustuv Manna

In recent years, layered kagome magnets have emerged as promising platforms for Berry curvature engineering and unconventional transport phenomena. Here, we present the single-crystal growth, magnetization, and electrical transport characterizations of the van der Waals-like layered antiferromagnet …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134437] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:

In recent years, layered kagome magnets have emerged as promising platforms for Berry curvature engineering and unconventional transport phenomena. Here, we present the single-crystal growth, magnetization, and electrical transport characterizations of the van der Waals-like layered antiferromagnet …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134437] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026

item.title: Self-$G$-ality in $1+1$ dimensions
item.description:
Author(s): Takamasa Ando

We explore topological manipulations in one spatial dimension, which are defined for a system with a global symmetry and map the system to another one with a dual symmetry. In particular, we discuss fusion category symmetries enhanced by the invariance of the actions of topological manipulations, i.…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155144] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Takamasa Ando

We explore topological manipulations in one spatial dimension, which are defined for a system with a global symmetry and map the system to another one with a dual symmetry. In particular, we discuss fusion category symmetries enhanced by the invariance of the actions of topological manipulations, i.…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155144] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026

item.title: Quantum geometric contribution to the diffusion constant in metals and semimetals
item.description:
Author(s): A. A. Burkov

We discuss the quantum geometric contribution to the diffusion constant and the DC conductivity in metals and semimetals with linear Dirac dispersion. We demonstrate that, for systems with perfectly linear dispersion, there exists a clear and rigorous separation of the quantum geometric from the ord…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155145] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. A. Burkov

We discuss the quantum geometric contribution to the diffusion constant and the DC conductivity in metals and semimetals with linear Dirac dispersion. We demonstrate that, for systems with perfectly linear dispersion, there exists a clear and rigorous separation of the quantum geometric from the ord…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155145] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026

item.title: Mobile impurity in the Kitaev chain: Phase diagram and signatures of topology
item.description:
Author(s): A. V. Sadovnikov, M. S. Bahovadinov, A. N. Rubtsov, and A. A. Markov

We study the physics of a mobile impurity immersed in a one-dimensional topological superconductor. We discuss the phase diagram of this system obtained with exact diagonalization and Chevy-type variational Ansatz. We argue that the character of the transition from a weak to strong coupling regime d…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155146] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. V. Sadovnikov, M. S. Bahovadinov, A. N. Rubtsov, and A. A. Markov

We study the physics of a mobile impurity immersed in a one-dimensional topological superconductor. We discuss the phase diagram of this system obtained with exact diagonalization and Chevy-type variational Ansatz. We argue that the character of the transition from a weak to strong coupling regime d…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155146] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Mobile impurity in the Kitaev chain: Phase diagram and signatures of topology

item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Tunable Josephson diode effect mediated by topological surface states in ${\mathrm{BiSbTeSe}}_{2}$ Josephson junctions
item.description:
Author(s): Si-Li Wu, Zhi-Hui Ren, Peng Zhu, Hao-Chen Zhang, Xue-Tao Di, Chong Wang, Chuan Li, Zhiwei Wang, Cai-Zhen Li, and Zhi-Min Liao

Here, the authors demonstrate a gate-tunable Josephson diode effect (JDE) mediated by topological surface states in high-transparency Nb/BiSbTeSe2/Nb junctions. Temperature-dependent measurements show that the JDE is primarily driven by surface states, with bulk states contributing to the supercurrent but not to the diode effect. Gate tuning further enhances the surface contribution, increasing the diode efficiency from 40% to 50%. These findings establish topological surface states as a promising platform for efficient tunable superconducting diodes.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155435] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Si-Li Wu, Zhi-Hui Ren, Peng Zhu, Hao-Chen Zhang, Xue-Tao Di, Chong Wang, Chuan Li, Zhiwei Wang, Cai-Zhen Li, and Zhi-Min Liao

Here, the authors demonstrate a gate-tunable Josephson diode effect (JDE) mediated by topological surface states in high-transparency Nb/BiSbTeSe2/Nb junctions. Temperature-dependent measurements show that the JDE is primarily driven by surface states, with bulk states contributing to the supercurrent but not to the diode effect. Gate tuning further enhances the surface contribution, increasing the diode efficiency from 40% to 50%. These findings establish topological surface states as a promising platform for efficient tunable superconducting diodes.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155435] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Tunable Josephson diode effect mediated by topological surface states in ${\mathrm{BiSbTeSe}}_{2}$ Josephson junctions
item.dc:creator: Si-Li Wu, Zhi-Hui Ren, Peng Zhu, Hao-Chen Zhang, Xue-Tao Di, Chong Wang, Chuan Li, Zhiwei Wang, Cai-Zhen Li, and Zhi-Min Liao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Fermion parity and quantum capacitance oscillation with partially separated Majorana and quasi-Majorana modes
item.description:
Author(s): Tudor D. Stanescu and Sumanta Tewari

Quantum capacitance measurements of a hybrid semiconductor-superconductor nanowire coupled to a quantum dot can enable the determination of the shared fermion parity of Majorana zero modes. Here, the authors demonstrate that, in general, the observation of the corresponding parity-dependent quantum capacitance oscillations, as observed in recent experiments, indicates the presence of partially separated Majorana modes or topologically trivial quasi-Majorana modes, and does not represent a unique signature of topologically protected Majorana zero modes.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165422] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tudor D. Stanescu and Sumanta Tewari

Quantum capacitance measurements of a hybrid semiconductor-superconductor nanowire coupled to a quantum dot can enable the determination of the shared fermion parity of Majorana zero modes. Here, the authors demonstrate that, in general, the observation of the corresponding parity-dependent quantum capacitance oscillations, as observed in recent experiments, indicates the presence of partially separated Majorana modes or topologically trivial quasi-Majorana modes, and does not represent a unique signature of topologically protected Majorana zero modes.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165422] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Fermion parity and quantum capacitance oscillation with partially separated Majorana and quasi-Majorana modes
item.dc:creator: Tudor D. Stanescu and Sumanta Tewari
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Symmetry-governed flat band fragmentation in a kagome moiré quasicrystal
item.description:
Author(s): Yongdao Chen, Laiyuan Su, Yifan Gao, Lede Xian, and Li Huang

Moiré quasicrystals extend the twistronics paradigm beyond crystalline order, hosting electronic properties dictated by nonperiodic symmetry. Here, we investigate the fate of the kagome lattice's defining feature, its flat band, in a moiré quasicrystal formed by a ${30}^{∘}$-twisted bilayer. Our tig…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165423] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yongdao Chen, Laiyuan Su, Yifan Gao, Lede Xian, and Li Huang

Moiré quasicrystals extend the twistronics paradigm beyond crystalline order, hosting electronic properties dictated by nonperiodic symmetry. Here, we investigate the fate of the kagome lattice's defining feature, its flat band, in a moiré quasicrystal formed by a 30∘-twisted bilayer. Our tight-bind…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165423] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Symmetry-governed flat band fragmentation in a kagome moiré quasicrystal

item.title: $1s2p$ resonant inelastic x-ray scattering natural circular dichroism
item.description:
Author(s): James M. Ablett, Nazanin Kordestani, Amélie Juhin, Alessandro De Frenza, Marie-Anne Arrio, Patrick Rosa, Elen Duverger-Nédellec, Edwige Otero, Jean-Pascal Rueff, Philippe Sainctavit, and Elizabeth A. Hillard

Natural circular dichroism (NCD) provides fundamental insight into molecular chirality and stereochemical environments. Extending this concept into the hard-x-ray regime enables element-specific and site-selective chiral sensitivity inaccessible to optical wavelengths. Here, we demonstrate that $1s2…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161116] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026

Natural circular dichroism (NCD) provides fundamental insight into molecular chirality and stereochemical environments. Extending this concept into the hard-x-ray regime enables element-specific and site-selective chiral sensitivity inaccessible to optical wavelengths. Here, we demonstrate that 1s2p…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161116] Published Tue Apr 21, 2026

item.title: Three-dimensional spin susceptibility in ${\mathrm{Ba}}_{0.75}{\mathrm{K}}_{0.25}{\mathrm{Fe}}_{2}{\mathrm{As}}_{2}$: Out-of-plane modulation revealed by neutron spectroscopy and theoretical modeling
item.description:
Author(s): Naoki Murai, Katsuhiro Suzuki, Masamichi Nakajima, Maiko Kofu, Seiko Ohira-Kawamura, Yasuhiro Inamura, and Ryoichi Kajimoto

We present a combined experimental and theoretical investigation of the spin dynamics in the iron-based superconductor ${\mathrm{Ba}}_{0.75}{\mathrm{K}}_{0.25}{\mathrm{Fe}}_{2}{\mathrm{As}}_{2}$. Time-of-flight inelastic neutron scattering measurements reveal the three-dimensional (3D) nature of the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134430] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Naoki Murai, Katsuhiro Suzuki, Masamichi Nakajima, Maiko Kofu, Seiko Ohira-Kawamura, Yasuhiro Inamura, and Ryoichi Kajimoto

We present a combined experimental and theoretical investigation of the spin dynamics in the iron-based superconductor Ba0.75K0.25Fe2As2. Time-of-flight inelastic neutron scattering measurements reveal the three-dimensional (3D) nature of the spin fluctuations, manifested as out-of-plane modulations…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134430] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

item.title: Noncollinear ferromagnetism in the Kondo-lattice compound ${\mathrm{Ce}}_{5}{\mathrm{CoGe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Jinyu Wu, Jiawen Zhang, Toni Shiroka, Shams Sohel Islam, Mingyi Wang, Yongjun Zhang, Devashibhai T. Adroja, Yu Liu, Huiqiu Yuan, and Michael Smidman

The dense Kondo lattice ${\mathrm{Ce}}_{5}{\mathrm{CoGe}}_{2}$ exhibits superconductivity after the magnetic ordering is suppressed by pressure. Here the ambient pressure magnetic state is investigated via magnetization, heat capacity, powder neutron diffraction, and muon-spin relaxation measurement…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134431] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

Author(s): Jinyu Wu, Jiawen Zhang, Toni Shiroka, Shams Sohel Islam, Mingyi Wang, Yongjun Zhang, Devashibhai T. Adroja, Yu Liu, Huiqiu Yuan, and Michael Smidman

The dense Kondo lattice Ce5CoGe2 exhibits superconductivity after the magnetic ordering is suppressed by pressure. Here the ambient pressure magnetic state is investigated via magnetization, heat capacity, powder neutron diffraction, and muon-spin relaxation measurements. Neutron diffraction results…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134431] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: Rashba spin-orbit coupling and artificially engineered topological superconductors
item.description:
Author(s): Sankar Das Sarma, Katharina Laubscher, Haining Pan, Jay D. Sau, and Tudor D. Stanescu

Rashba spin–orbit coupling (SOC), introduced in seminal works by Emmanuel Rashba, plays a central role in engineered topological superconductivity. In this Perspective, the authors review how Rashba SOC enables the realization of low-dimensional topological superconductors supporting Majorana zero modes, the key building blocks for topological quantum computation. They highlight how tuning the Rashba SOC can enhance the topological gap, and survey material platforms and design strategies where Rashba SOC is leveraged to create and control topological superconducting phases.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 139604] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sankar Das Sarma, Katharina Laubscher, Haining Pan, Jay D. Sau, and Tudor D. Stanescu

Rashba spin–orbit coupling (SOC), introduced in seminal works by Emmanuel Rashba, plays a central role in engineered topological superconductivity. In this Perspective, the authors review how Rashba SOC enables the realization of low-dimensional topological superconductors supporting Majorana zero modes, the key building blocks for topological quantum computation. They highlight how tuning the Rashba SOC can enhance the topological gap, and survey material platforms and design strategies where Rashba SOC is leveraged to create and control topological superconducting phases.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 139604] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Rashba spin-orbit coupling and artificially engineered topological superconductors
item.dc:creator: Sankar Das Sarma, Katharina Laubscher, Haining Pan, Jay D. Sau, and Tudor D. Stanescu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Technical Perspective
item.prism:section: Technical Perspective

item.title: Hidden magnon Berry curvature drives vertical magnon transport
item.description:
Author(s): Atul Rathor, Sahanawaj Akhtar, and Arijit Haldar

The authors show here that quasi-two-dimensional magnetic insulators comprising a few layers are fundamentally distinct, at the quantum geometric level, from perfectly flat single-layered 2D magnets. They discover that magnon excitations in such quasi-2D insulators experience in-plane components of a bosonic symplectic Berry curvature, absent in flat 2D magnetic insulators. They call this the “hidden magnon Berry curvature” (HMBC). They propose that HMBC can generate (and be detected via) magnon transport perpendicular to the layers of van der Waals materials in response to thermal or magnetic-field gradients. Their work points toward the possibility of yet-undiscovered hidden quantum geometric transport in layered quasi-2D magnets.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144426] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Atul Rathor, Sahanawaj Akhtar, and Arijit Haldar

The authors show here that quasi-two-dimensional magnetic insulators comprising a few layers are fundamentally distinct, at the quantum geometric level, from perfectly flat single-layered 2D magnets. They discover that magnon excitations in such quasi-2D insulators experience in-plane components of a bosonic symplectic Berry curvature, absent in flat 2D magnetic insulators. They call this the “hidden magnon Berry curvature” (HMBC). They propose that HMBC can generate (and be detected via) magnon transport perpendicular to the layers of van der Waals materials in response to thermal or magnetic-field gradients. Their work points toward the possibility of yet-undiscovered hidden quantum geometric transport in layered quasi-2D magnets.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144426] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Hidden magnon Berry curvature drives vertical magnon transport
item.dc:creator: Atul Rathor, Sahanawaj Akhtar, and Arijit Haldar

item.title: Transport of Dirac magnons driven by gauge fields
item.description:
Author(s): Luis Fernández, Ka Shen, Leandro O. Nascimento, Van Sérgio Alves, Roberto E. Troncoso, and Nicolas Vidal-Silva

We present a unified quantum field theory for Dirac magnons coupled to emergent gauge fields. At zero temperature, any space- and time-dependent gauge perturbation drives magnons out of equilibrium, generating spin currents and magnon accumulation without conventional thermal or chemical-potential g…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144427] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

At zero temperature, any space- and time-dependent gauge perturbation drives magnons out of equilibrium, generating spin currents and magnon accumulation without conventional thermal or chemical-potential g…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144427] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

item.title: Pathways from a chiral superconductor to a composite Fermi liquid
item.description:
Author(s): Yunchao Zhang, Leyna Shackleton, and T. Senthil

Recent experiments have reported chiral time-reversal broken superconductivity in $n$-layer rhombohedral graphene for $n=4,\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}5,\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}\text{and}\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}6$. Introducing a moiré potential by alignment with a hexagonal boron nitride sub…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144510] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yunchao Zhang, Leyna Shackleton, and T. Senthil

Recent experiments have reported chiral time-reversal broken superconductivity in n-layer rhombohedral graphene for n=4,5,and6. Introducing a moiré potential by alignment with a hexagonal boron nitride substrate suppresses the superconductivity but leads instead to various fractional quantum anomalo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144510] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Pathways from a chiral superconductor to a composite Fermi liquid

item.title: Construction of asymptotic quantum many-body scar states in the $\mathrm{SU}(N)$ Hubbard model
item.description:

We construct asymptotic quantum many-body scars (AQMBS) in one-dimensional $\mathrm{SU}(N)$ Hubbard chains ($N≥3$) by embedding the scar subspace into an auxiliary Hilbert subspace ${\mathcal{H}}_{P}$ and identifying a parent Hamiltonian within it, together with a corresponding extension of the rest…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155137] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

We construct asymptotic quantum many-body scars (AQMBS) in one-dimensional SU(N) Hubbard chains (N≥3) by embedding the scar subspace into an auxiliary Hilbert subspace HP and identifying a parent Hamiltonian within it, together with a corresponding extension of the restricted spectrum-generating alg…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155137] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

item.title: Higher-dimensional generalization of the Kitaev spin liquid
item.description:
Author(s): Po-Jui Chen and Piers Coleman

We construct an exactly solvable model of a four-dimensional Kitaev spin liquid. The lattice structure is orthorhombic and each unit cell contains six sublattice degrees of freedom. We demonstrate that the Fermi surface of the model is made up of two-dimensional surfaces. Additionally, we evaluate t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155140] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

Author(s): Po-Jui Chen and Piers Coleman

We construct an exactly solvable model of a four-dimensional Kitaev spin liquid. The lattice structure is orthorhombic and each unit cell contains six sublattice degrees of freedom. We demonstrate that the Fermi surface of the model is made up of two-dimensional surfaces. Additionally, we evaluate t…

item.title: Optical response of the Dirac semimetals ${\mathrm{GdSb}}_{0.56}{\mathrm{Te}}_{1.35}$ and ${\mathrm{GdSb}}_{0.45}{\mathrm{Te}}_{1.50}$: Influence of charge density wave distortion and magnetic order
item.description:
Author(s): S. Rojewski, R. Borkenhagen, Runhan Li, Diego García Ovalle, Stepan S. Tsirkin, Harish Kumar, K. Raju, Raman Sankar, S. Lei, L. M. Schoop, Maia G. Vergniory, and C. A. Kuntscher

The square-net materials ${\mathrm{GdSb}}_{x}{\mathrm{Te}}_{2−x−δ}$ provide a platform for exploring the effects of charge density wave (CDW) distortion and band filling on the topological electronic band structure. In this work we use infrared spectroscopy to probe the temperature-dependent electro…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155141] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): S. Rojewski, R. Borkenhagen, Runhan Li, Diego García Ovalle, Stepan S. Tsirkin, Harish Kumar, K. Raju, Raman Sankar, S. Lei, L. M. Schoop, Maia G. Vergniory, and C. A. Kuntscher

The square-net materials GdSbxTe2−x−δ provide a platform for exploring the effects of charge density wave (CDW) distortion and band filling on the topological electronic band structure. In this work we use infrared spectroscopy to probe the temperature-dependent electronic excitations in GdSb0.56Te1…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155141] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Optical response of the Dirac semimetals ${\mathrm{GdSb}}_{0.56}{\mathrm{Te}}_{1.35}$ and ${\mathrm{GdSb}}_{0.45}{\mathrm{Te}}_{1.50}$: Influence of charge density wave distortion and magnetic order
item.dc:creator: S. Rojewski, R. Borkenhagen, Runhan Li, Diego García Ovalle, Stepan S. Tsirkin, Harish Kumar, K. Raju, Raman Sankar, S. Lei, L. M. Schoop, Maia G. Vergniory, and C. A. Kuntscher

item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Symmetry-driven spin anisotropic magnetotransport in the quantum spin Hall insulator $1{T}^{′}\text{−}{\mathrm{WTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Shrushti Tapar, Bent Weber, Saroj P. Dash, Shantanu Mukherjee, and Bhaskaran Muralidharan

We present a comprehensive analysis of magnetotransport across monolayer $1{T}^{′}\text{−}{\mathrm{WTe}}_{2}$, highlighting the role of nonsymmorphic symmetries in governing edge-state spin behavior. By comparing the electronic transmission in nanoribbons with edges along the crystallographic $\stac…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155431] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shrushti Tapar, Bent Weber, Saroj P. Dash, Shantanu Mukherjee, and Bhaskaran Muralidharan

We present a comprehensive analysis of magnetotransport across monolayer 1T′−WTe2, highlighting the role of nonsymmorphic symmetries in governing edge-state spin behavior. By comparing the electronic transmission in nanoribbons with edges along the crystallographic ŷ and x̂ directions, our analysis…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155431] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Symmetry-driven spin anisotropic magnetotransport in the quantum spin Hall insulator $1{T}^{′}\text{−}{\mathrm{WTe}}_{2}$

item.title: Spin-orbit coupling engineered higher-order topology and flat bands in two-dimensional Su-Schrieffer-Heeger models
item.description:
Author(s): Yi-Ju Gou, Hong Wu, Qing-Xu Li, Jian Li, and Jia-Ji Zhu

We demonstrate how spin-orbit coupling (SOC) controls band topology and induces higher-order topological corner states in a two-dimensional Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) model. SOC lifts spin degeneracy and, when applied along both lattice directions, generates robust zero-energy corner states. By matc…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155433] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yi-Ju Gou, Hong Wu, Qing-Xu Li, Jian Li, and Jia-Ji Zhu

We demonstrate how spin-orbit coupling (SOC) controls band topology and induces higher-order topological corner states in a two-dimensional Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) model. SOC lifts spin degeneracy and, when applied along both lattice directions, generates robust zero-energy corner states. By matc…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155433] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

item.title: Symmetry-directed electronic and optical properties in a two-dimensional square-lattice ZnPc metal-organic framework
item.description:
Author(s): Zhonghui Han, Lanting Feng, Guodong Yu, and Shengjun Yuan

The electronic structure of materials is fundamentally governed by their crystal symmetry. While most research on two-dimensional materials has focused on hexagonal lattices, such as graphene, hexagonal boron nitride, and transition metal dichalcogenides. This work explores a square-lattice system: …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165417] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

Author(s): Zhonghui Han, Lanting Feng, Guodong Yu, and Shengjun Yuan

The electronic structure of materials is fundamentally governed by their crystal symmetry. While most research on two-dimensional materials has focused on hexagonal lattices, such as graphene, hexagonal boron nitride, and transition metal dichalcogenides. This work explores a square-lattice system: …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165417] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

item.title: Layer Hall effect induced by altermagnetism
item.description:
Author(s): Fang Qin (覃昉) and Rui Chen

In this work, we propose a scheme to realize the layer Hall effect in the ferromagnetic topological insulator ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Se}}_{3}$ via proximity to $d$-wave altermagnets. We show that an altermagnet and an in-plane magnetic field applied near one surface gap the corresponding Dirac c…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165418] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Fang Qin (覃昉) and Rui Chen

In this work, we propose a scheme to realize the layer Hall effect in the ferromagnetic topological insulator Bi2Se3 via proximity to d-wave altermagnets. We show that an altermagnet and an in-plane magnetic field applied near one surface gap the corresponding Dirac cone, yielding an altermagnet-ind…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165418] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

item.title: Visible and terahertz nonlinear responses in the topological noble metal dichalcogenide ${\mathrm{PdTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): George J. de Coster, Lucas Lafeta, Stefan Heiserer, Cormac Ó Coileáin, Zdenek Sofer, Achim Hartschuh, Georg S. Duesberg, and Paul Seifert

Nonlinear processes can offer pathways to next-generation sensors and frequency mixing devices to overcome modern imaging, detection, and communication challenges. In this article, we report on strong second- and third-order nonlinear optical responses in visible and terahertz light in single crysta…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165419] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

In this article, we report on strong second- and third-order nonlinear optical responses in visible and terahertz light in single crysta…

item.title: Majorana zero modes in semiconductor-superconductor hybrid structures: Defining topology in short and disordered nanowires through Majorana splitting
item.description:
Author(s): Haining Pan and Sankar Das Sarma

Majorana zero modes (MZMs) are bound midgap topological excitations at the ends of a one-dimensional (1D) topological superconductor, which must come in pairs. If the two MZMs in the pair are sufficiently well-separated by a distance much larger than their individual localization lengths, then the M…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165420] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

Author(s): Haining Pan and Sankar Das Sarma

Majorana zero modes (MZMs) are bound midgap topological excitations at the ends of a one-dimensional (1D) topological superconductor, which must come in pairs. If the two MZMs in the pair are sufficiently well-separated by a distance much larger than their individual localization lengths, then the M…

item.title: Quantized spin Hall conductivity in altermagnetic ${\mathrm{Fe}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{2}\mathrm{O}$ with mirror-spin coupling
item.description:
Author(s): Run-Wu Zhang, Chaoxi Cui, Yang Wang, Jingyi Duan, Zhi-Ming Yu, and Yugui Yao

Due to spin-orbit coupling (SOC), a quantized spin Hall conductivity has not yet been reported in realistic materials. Here, we tackle this challenge by predicting robust quantized spin Hall conductivity in monolayer ${\mathrm{Fe}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{2}\mathrm{O}$. The underlying physics originates f…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161115] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Run-Wu Zhang, Chaoxi Cui, Yang Wang, Jingyi Duan, Zhi-Ming Yu, and Yugui Yao

Due to spin-orbit coupling (SOC), a quantized spin Hall conductivity has not yet been reported in realistic materials. Here, we tackle this challenge by predicting robust quantized spin Hall conductivity in monolayer Fe2Te2O. The underlying physics originates from the unrecognized mirror-spin coupli…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161115] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantized spin Hall conductivity in altermagnetic ${\mathrm{Fe}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{2}\mathrm{O}$ with mirror-spin coupling

item.title: Adiabatic charge transport through non-Bloch bands
item.description:
Author(s): Dharana Joshi and Tanay Nag

We explore the nonreciprocal intracell hopping mediated non-Hermitian topological phases of an extended Su-Schrieffer-Heeger model hosting second-nearest-neighbor hopping. We microscopically analyze the phase boundaries using the non-Bloch momentum while the off-critical (critical) phases are direct…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161408] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

We microscopically analyze the phase boundaries using the non-Bloch momentum while the off-critical (critical) phases are direct…

item.title: Worldline deconfinement and emergent long-range interaction in the entanglement Hamiltonian and in the entanglement spectrum
item.description:
Author(s): Zenan Liu, Zhe Wang, Dao-Xin Yao, and Zheng Yan

The entanglement spectrum (ES) is a powerful tool for probing topological phases. While its behavior in gapped systems is well understood, its properties in gapless regimes remain unclear. In this work, we employ a quantum Monte Carlo method to study the ES of a two-dimensional square-octagon lattic…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144425] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026

The entanglement spectrum (ES) is a powerful tool for probing topological phases. While its behavior in gapped systems is well understood, its properties in gapless regimes remain unclear. In this work, we employ a quantum Monte Carlo method to study the ES of a two-dimensional square-octagon lattic…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144425] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026

item.title: Interplay of magnetism and topological band robustness in layered rare-earth diantimonides
item.description:
Author(s): Zhian Xu, Ze Yan, Jian Yuan, Kaige Wu, Wei Xia, Xia Wang, Chuanying Xi, Li Pi, Yuzhou Zhao, Shihao Zhang, and Yanfeng Guo

Layered compounds $R\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{b}}_{2}$ ($R$ = light rare earth), which first hosted the discovery of non-rivial topological fermions in nonmagnetic $\mathrm{La}\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{b}}_{2}$, offer an exceptional platform for probing the interplay between magnetism and topological electronic s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155135] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhian Xu, Ze Yan, Jian Yuan, Kaige Wu, Wei Xia, Xia Wang, Chuanying Xi, Li Pi, Yuzhou Zhao, Shihao Zhang, and Yanfeng Guo

Layered compounds RSb2 (R = light rare earth), which first hosted the discovery of non-rivial topological fermions in nonmagnetic LaSb2, offer an exceptional platform for probing the interplay between magnetism and topological electronic states. Through high-field magnetotransport measurements and f…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155135] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Interplay of magnetism and topological band robustness in layered rare-earth diantimonides

item.title: Various electronic crystal phases in rhombohedral graphene multilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Wangqian Miao and Chu Li

We systematically investigate the emergence of electron crystal phases in rhombohedral multilayer graphene using comprehensive self-consistent Hartree Fock calculations combined with ab initio tight binding model. As the carrier density increases, we uncover an isospin cascade sequence of phase tran…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155136] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026

item.title: Laser-induced commensurate-incommensurate transition of charge order in a Hubbard superlattice
item.description:
Author(s): Hua Chai, Zhenyu Cheng, Qinxin Hu, Zhongbing Huang, Xiang Hu, Xuedong Tian, and Liang Du

We investigate the nonequilibrium dynamics of charge density waves in a pumped one-dimensional Hubbard superlattice with staggered on-site Coulomb interactions at half filling, using time-dependent exact diagonalization. In equilibrium, the system exhibits commensurate charge correlations consistent…

item.title: Hund-projected Kanamori model: An effective description of Hund's metals near the Mott insulating regime
item.description:
Author(s): Johan Carlström

Hund's coupling plays a decisive role in shaping electron correlations of multi-orbital systems, giving rise to a class of materials—Hund's metals—that combine local-moment physics with metallic transport. Here, we derive an effective low-energy description of such a system near the Mott insulating …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165135] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Johan Carlström

Hund's coupling plays a decisive role in shaping electron correlations of multi-orbital systems, giving rise to a class of materials—Hund's metals—that combine local-moment physics with metallic transport. Here, we derive an effective low-energy description of such a system near the Mott insulating …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165135] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026

item.description:

We investigate the topological phase diagram of an extension of the Haldane model with equal spin pairing superconductivity. In two dimensions, we find a topological nodal superconducting phase, which exhibits a chiral Majorana mode propagating along the edges of nanoribbons with cylindrical boundar…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165416] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Random singlet physics in the exchange-disordered two-dimensional triangular material ${\mathrm{YbCu}}_{1.14}{\mathrm{Se}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Caitlin S. T. Kengle, S. M. Thomas, R. Movshovich, Shengzhi Zhang, Eun Sang Choi, Minseong Lee, P. F. S. Rosa, and A. O. Scheie

Disordered quantum spin liquid materials often display both quantum fluctuations and glass freezing at low temperatures, which are difficult to reconcile. The authors study here intrinsically disordered triangular YbCuxSe2 and show bulk properties strikingly similar to other triangular quantum spin liquid candidate compounds, especially a sublinear specific heat versus temperature. A phenomenological random singlet model is able to reproduce this signature, suggesting possibly universal behavior for quantum spin liquids with randomness in their spin interactions.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134427] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Caitlin S. T. Kengle, S. M. Thomas, R. Movshovich, Shengzhi Zhang, Eun Sang Choi, Minseong Lee, P. F. S. Rosa, and A. O. Scheie

Disordered quantum spin liquid materials often display both quantum fluctuations and glass freezing at low temperatures, which are difficult to reconcile. The authors study here intrinsically disordered triangular YbCuxSe2 and show bulk properties strikingly similar to other triangular quantum spin liquid candidate compounds, especially a sublinear specific heat versus temperature. A phenomenological random singlet model is able to reproduce this signature, suggesting possibly universal behavior for quantum spin liquids with randomness in their spin interactions.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134427] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Random singlet physics in the exchange-disordered two-dimensional triangular material ${\mathrm{YbCu}}_{1.14}{\mathrm{Se}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Caitlin S. T. Kengle, S. M. Thomas, R. Movshovich, Shengzhi Zhang, Eun Sang Choi, Minseong Lee, P. F. S. Rosa, and A. O. Scheie

item.title: Magnetic penetration depth in topological superconductors: Effect of Majorana surface states and application for ${\mathrm{UTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Kazuki Akuzawa, Jushin Tei, Ryoi Ohashi, Satoshi Fujimoto, and Takeshi Mizushima

In this study, we examine how orbital degrees of freedom and Majorana surface states influence the magnetic penetration depth in the superconductor ${\mathrm{UTe}}_{2}$. Using a two-orbital model, we analyze pairing states belonging to the irreducible representations of the ${D}_{2h}$ crystal symmet…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144506] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kazuki Akuzawa, Jushin Tei, Ryoi Ohashi, Satoshi Fujimoto, and Takeshi Mizushima

In this study, we examine how orbital degrees of freedom and Majorana surface states influence the magnetic penetration depth in the superconductor UTe2. Using a two-orbital model, we analyze pairing states belonging to the irreducible representations of the D2h crystal symmetry: Au, B1u, B2u, and B…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144506] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetic penetration depth in topological superconductors: Effect of Majorana surface states and application for ${\mathrm{UTe}}_{2}$

Understanding the interplay between electronic correlations and band topology remains a central challenge in condensed matter physics, primarily hindered by a language-mismatch problem. While band topology is naturally formulated within a single-particle band theory, strong correlations typically el…

Discoveries of both altermagnets and topological states have profoundly reshaped our understanding of condensed-matter physics and materials. However, tunable topological states in altermagnets remain largely unexplored. Here, we put forward the realization of second-order topological insulator (SOT…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155428] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026

item.title: Proximity-induced superconductivity and emerging topological phases in altermagnet-based heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): Ohidul Alam, Amartya Pal, Paramita Dutta, and Arijit Saha

We present a theoretical framework for investigating superconducting proximity effect in altermagnet (AM)–superconductor (SC) heterostructures. In general, AMs, characterized by vanishing net magnetization but spin-split electronic spectra, provide a promising platform for realizing unconventional q…

In general, AMs, characterized by vanishing net magnetization but spin-split electronic spectra, provide a promising platform for realizing unconventional q…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155429] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Proximity-induced superconductivity and emerging topological phases in altermagnet-based heterostructures
item.dc:creator: Ohidul Alam, Amartya Pal, Paramita Dutta, and Arijit Saha
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Multichannel Kondo effect in one-dimensional superconducting leads
item.description:
Author(s): Pradip Kattel, Abay Zhakenov, and Natan Andrei

The authors uncover here a rich phase diagram when a quantum spin is placed in an interacting superconducting system, with different phases arising due to the competition of the Kondo and superconducting interactions. Particularly surprising is the appearance of multichannel physics in a gapped superconducting phase. They further show that in each phase, the Hilbert space splits into several towers of excitations. By computing the impurity entropy explicitly in each phase, they show that the excitation towers induce interesting nonmonotonic behavior, which is experimentally accessible.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165130] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pradip Kattel, Abay Zhakenov, and Natan Andrei

The authors uncover here a rich phase diagram when a quantum spin is placed in an interacting superconducting system, with different phases arising due to the competition of the Kondo and superconducting interactions. Particularly surprising is the appearance of multichannel physics in a gapped superconducting phase. They further show that in each phase, the Hilbert space splits into several towers of excitations. By computing the impurity entropy explicitly in each phase, they show that the excitation towers induce interesting nonmonotonic behavior, which is experimentally accessible.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165130] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Multichannel Kondo effect in one-dimensional superconducting leads
item.dc:creator: Pradip Kattel, Abay Zhakenov, and Natan Andrei
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Connection between negative charge transfer and reduced on-site Coulomb energy in the correlated topological metal ${\mathrm{CoTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): A. R. Shelke, C.-W. Chuang, S. Hamamoto, M. Oura, M. Yoshimura, N. Hiraoka, C.-N. Kuo, C.-S. Lue, A. Fujimori, and A. Chainani

The layered $3d$ transition metal dichalcogenide ${\mathrm{CoTe}}_{2}$ is a topological Dirac type-II metal. However, the Co $3d$-bands in ${\mathrm{CoTe}}_{2}$ do not exhibit the expected correlation-induced band narrowing seen in CoO. We address this conundrum by studying the electronic structure …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165132] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. R. Shelke, C.-W. Chuang, S. Hamamoto, M. Oura, M. Yoshimura, N. Hiraoka, C.-N. Kuo, C.-S. Lue, A. Fujimori, and A. Chainani

The layered 3d transition metal dichalcogenide CoTe2 is a topological Dirac type-II metal. However, the Co 3d-bands in CoTe2 do not exhibit the expected correlation-induced band narrowing seen in CoO. We address this conundrum by studying the electronic structure of CoTe2 using hard x-ray photoemiss…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165132] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Connection between negative charge transfer and reduced on-site Coulomb energy in the correlated topological metal ${\mathrm{CoTe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: A. R. Shelke, C.-W. Chuang, S. Hamamoto, M. Oura, M. Yoshimura, N. Hiraoka, C.-N. Kuo, C.-S. Lue, A. Fujimori, and A. Chainani

item.title: Role of on-site Coulomb energy and negative charge transfer in the Dirac semimetal ${\mathrm{NiTe}}_{2}$

Angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy combined with band structure calculations have shown that the layered transition metal dichalcogenide ${\mathrm{NiTe}}_{2}$ is a type-II Dirac semimetal. However, conflicting conclusions were reported regarding the role of electron correlations in ${\mathrm{…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165133] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026

Author(s): A. R. Shelke, C.-W. Chuang, S. Hamamoto, M. Oura, M. Yoshimura, N. Hiraoka, C.-N. Kuo, C.-S. Lue, A. Fujimori, and A. Chainani

Angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy combined with band structure calculations have shown that the layered transition metal dichalcogenide NiTe2 is a type-II Dirac semimetal. However, conflicting conclusions were reported regarding the role of electron correlations in NiTe2. We study core level…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165133] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Role of on-site Coulomb energy and negative charge transfer in the Dirac semimetal ${\mathrm{NiTe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: A. R. Shelke, C.-W. Chuang, S. Hamamoto, M. Oura, M. Yoshimura, N. Hiraoka, C.-N. Kuo, C.-S. Lue, A. Fujimori, and A. Chainani

item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Spin-spiral instability of the Nagaoka ferromagnet in the crossover between square and triangular lattices
item.description:
Author(s): Darren Pereira and Erich J. Mueller

The hard-core Fermi-Hubbard model displays a massive spin degeneracy at half-filling: superexchange is forbidden in the hard-core limit, so all spin configurations have the same energy. Introducing a single hole, whose kinetic energy depends on the spin state, leads to magnetic ordering. This ordering is lattice dependent. The authors study here how it evolves as one interpolates between a square and triangular lattice, which can be explored using optical lattices. Using perturbative and variational arguments, they identify a phase transition between a ferromagnet and a spin-spiral state.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161114] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Darren Pereira and Erich J. Mueller

The hard-core Fermi-Hubbard model displays a massive spin degeneracy at half-filling: superexchange is forbidden in the hard-core limit, so all spin configurations have the same energy. Introducing a single hole, whose kinetic energy depends on the spin state, leads to magnetic ordering. This ordering is lattice dependent. The authors study here how it evolves as one interpolates between a square and triangular lattice, which can be explored using optical lattices. Using perturbative and variational arguments, they identify a phase transition between a ferromagnet and a spin-spiral state.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161114] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-spiral instability of the Nagaoka ferromagnet in the crossover between square and triangular lattices
item.dc:creator: Darren Pereira and Erich J. Mueller

item.title: Generalized Hellmann-Feynman perturbation theory: Applications to topological magnons
item.description:
Author(s): Qi-Hui Chen, Fei-Jie Huang, and Yong-Ping Fu

Conventional perturbation theory, developed for Hermitian matrices, is inadequate for determining the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of bosonic Bogoliubov–de Gennes (BdG) Hamiltonians, whose dynamical structure leads to a non-Hermitian generalized eigenvalue problem. Here we extend the Hellmann-Feynma…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134424] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026

Conventional perturbation theory, developed for Hermitian matrices, is inadequate for determining the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of bosonic Bogoliubov–de Gennes (BdG) Hamiltonians, whose dynamical structure leads to a non-Hermitian generalized eigenvalue problem. Here we extend the Hellmann-Feynma…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134424] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
